goals.target: Remote clients integrate through FastAPI /api/v1 instead of Node internals.
Mobile-first Next.js PWA shell uses only /api/v1 calls.
Live worker browser sessions are represented as remote sandbox sessions, with hosted provider readiness separate from local CDP proof.
Operator dashboard exposes API, browser, safety, and visual-test readiness.
All future phases use one Cortex-canonical RALPH loop, role-separated execution, non-mocked proof labels, dashboard scoring, and PR-based memory visibility.
Phase 33 introduces typed CaseState, G0-G8 universal gate skeleton, PEMS maturity schema, and shadow-mode procedural reconstruction without letting it drive healthcare answers.
Phase 34 persists final shadow runs and accumulates PEMS candidate maturity from real graph traces while keeping procedural candidates non-driving.
Phase 35 requires explicit reviewer, validator, citation, and safety gates before a PEMS candidate can enter supervised advisory mode; production driving remains disabled.
Phase 36 lets evaluator draft notes and NeSTR-style consistency traces advise reviewers while human and deterministic review gates remain authoritative.
Phase 37 exposes an operator-facing reviewer UI for ref-only advisory material and explicit approve/reject/block review actions.
Phase 38 renders deterministic-vs-advisory comparison rows, cited evidence chips, and live-gated evaluator provenance without automatic production recommendations.
Phase 39 creates live-gated advisory drafts only through observed egress and adds reviewer filters without counting mocked LLM output as proof.
Phase 40 labels live evaluator advisory claims as supported, low confidence, or unsupported and makes unsupported claims a visible reviewer veto without creating evidence.
Phase 41 persists reviewer claim edits as ref-only revision records, preserves before/after hashes, and re-runs deterministic closure without creating evidence.
Phase 42 binds reviewer claim revision records to later explicit review decisions and follow-up workflow state without enabling production recommendations.
Phase 43 exports longitudinal reviewer history refs and hashes across drafts, revisions, reviews, and follow-ups without storing raw history or enabling production recommendations.
Phase 44 lets operators search, sort, and compare reviewer history export snapshots across longer windows without creating evidence or production recommendations.
Docker Compose defines the Node runtime, FastAPI connector, Next.js PWA, and FalkorDB dependency services.
Node connector image can run real Graphiti/FalkorDB product memory when credentials enable the adapter.
Docker Compose defines a Postgres transactional storage target while the current app runtime remains safely on SQLite until migration tests pass.
A dedicated compose override selects Postgres runtime through a Docker-secret database URL without bypassing proof gates.
Hosted/WebRTC browser sandbox provider can replace local CDP without changing the public /api/v1 browser contract.
A provider-selection/preflight gate chooses a live sandbox candidate without storing provider URLs or secrets in Git.
Private provider config, selected provider, endpoint, and auth refs can be preflighted before live hosted browser enablement.
A selected real hosted provider must pass create-session, stream, screenshot/OCR, takeover, input, offsite fail-closed, teardown, and GUI/OCR proof before hosted readiness scores.
Self-hosted Steel Browser proves the selected-provider lifecycle locally without exposing raw frames, OCR text, endpoints, or input values.
Self-hosted Steel Browser operations are hardened for loopback-only networking, cleanup, retention, monitoring, digest-pinned images, and no hosted-readiness overclaim.
Remote self-hosted Steel must prove TLS API access, private CDP tunnel, host firewall defense in depth, and ten-check lifecycle proof before remote-host readiness scores.
Remote self-hosted Steel operations must prove patch cadence, backup/restore drill, health alerting, and on-call handoff after remote-host readiness.
WebRTC live-block signaling must exchange opaque offer/answer/ICE refs before WebRTC hosted readiness scores.
Operator-supplied dashboard/mobile live-block visual and OCR proof must replay from a private manifest before hosted readiness scores.
Operator launch readiness aggregates private config, live provider, WebRTC, visual/OCR, and final enablement gates without leaking secrets.
Real selected-provider private launch execution must be explicitly gated and human-reviewed before final hosted readiness can score.
The hosted adapter lifecycle can be contract-tested without provider credentials or live frames.
The hosted provider HTTP create-session adapter can be exercised against a local provider-compatible harness without leaking secrets.
The hosted provider stream, screenshot/OCR, takeover, input, teardown, and offsite-fail-closed lifecycle can be contract-tested before live provider enablement.
Encrypted durable graph checkpoints, enforced egress default, bound-parameter local store, and retention sweeper proof gate.
Registry-driven multi-skill selection, bounded worker breadth, and masked procedural worker memory feeding PEMS without answer driving.
Reviewer-approved matured PEMS skills may drive answers only through citation rails, demotion, kill switch, and privacy namespacing.
Pilot readiness packages live-model-default PWA, FastAPI/Node endpoint inventory, OpenClaw readiness, DB health, AWS communication, and Graphiti status into one non-PHI proof gate.
Memory skill-tree selector keeps DB authoritative while Graphiti/Zep supports non-standard journeys, procedural loops, and reviewer-gated skill consolidation.
Reviewer-approved mature memory candidates become PR-ready generated skill packages without auto-merge or production-driving authority.
Generated skill PR packages persist in a reviewer queue and produce an explicit non-auto-running PR executor plan after approval.
Approved generated skill queue items expose a human-operated dry-run executor surface before any branch, file write, or PR action can occur.
Regular-user MVP readiness is separated from production completeness, with PWA, API, DB, OpenClaw, Graphiti, dashboard, and blockers visible.

checks.nextAction: Open the member portal in the dedicated OpenClaw browser profile and sign in manually. Leave the authenticated tab open.

oldestPending.last_error: Graphiti bridge failed with exit 1: {"ok": false, "contractVersion": "2026-05-27.graphiti-product-memory.v1", "error": "Error 61 connecting to localhost:6380. Connection refused.", "errorType": "ConnectionError"}

externalReadiness.awsProofSource: sanitized phase59 smoke artifact via aws sts --profile phase30

productMemoryFactPreviews.preview: Prior safe cases found a plan-specific exception pattern for nonstandard imaging review.

steps.title: Current Feature And Case State Evaluation
steps.objective: Bind the request to DB user/session records, deterministic safety policy, available skills, and source-pointer evidence.

steps.title: Target Feature Coding Planning
steps.objective: Choose the nearest skill-tree route and define missing evidence, worker tasks, verification sensors, and fallback path.

steps.title: Code Or Worker Implementation
steps.objective: Execute only bounded read-only subtasks or generate a reviewer-only skill candidate; do not widen approval scopes.

steps.title: Code Testing
steps.objective: Run deterministic validators, citation/source-pointer checks, policy checks, and skill contract checks.

steps.title: Dashboard, MVP, And Visual/OCR Sensor Testing
steps.objective: Capture dashboard/API/UI state as refs only; never persist raw frame or OCR text.

steps.title: Separated Agentic Goal Evaluation Score
steps.objective: Score the loop against target thresholds and route pass/fail to repeat or promote.

steps.title: Pass Score Decision
steps.objective: If no, restart the loop with a smaller target. If yes, promote to supervised advisory or trusted answer-driving only through existing gates.

skillServerDraft.title: Acme New Plan non_standard_plan_document_review memory-derived journey

literatureAlignment.reflexion: episodic feedback informs later trials, but deterministic validators decide promotion
literatureAlignment.generativeAgents: retrieved experiences are reflected into higher-level procedural cues
literatureAlignment.voyager: skill library growth is gated by execution feedback and verification
literatureAlignment.coala: working, episodic, semantic, and procedural memory stay separated by role

commands: write generated skill files from the reviewed package

blockers: Postgres production/default rollout remains a production blocker; SQLite is acceptable for local MVP proof.
Graphiti/Zep memory is degraded and remains advisory/degraded until live schema proof is green.
Hosted/remote browser production readiness is not fully green; local/contract proof remains separate.
Authenticated live OpenClaw portal proof is still live-gated and requires a user-controlled signed-in session.
checks.recommendation: Use as a local/pilot MVP with visible degraded production dependencies; fix blockers before production launch.

Static MVP remains the compatibility harness until PWA parity.
Next.js mobile viewport visual test passed.
Worker Browser live block rendered a data:image/jpeg frame through FastAPI /api/v1.
Canonical goal-tied RALPH operating system is mirrored in repo docs and must be mirrored in Cortex before the phase is done.
Typed CaseState, G0-G8 gate skeleton, PEMS maturity schema, and shadow reconstruction are implemented in a deterministic module.
Phase 35 PEMS supervised promotion gate exposes reviewer, validator, citation, safety, and production-disabled proof.
Phase 36 reviewer/evaluator workbench exposes sanitized advisory draft notes, consistency trace refs, and explicit human-review linkage.
Phase 37 reviewer UI renders ref-only advisory drafts and approve/reject/block controls backed by explicit review writes.
Phase 38 reviewer comparison panel renders deterministic-vs-advisory rows, source-pointer chips, and evaluator provenance without claiming production authority.
Phase 39 reviewer workbench can filter drafts and only marks live evaluator proof when observed egress created a non-mocked advisory draft.
Phase 40 reviewer workbench labels advisory claims, shows source-pointer support, and vetoes unsupported or low-confidence claims without creating evidence.
Phase 41 reviewer workbench records before/after claim revisions, deterministic reclosure, source-pointer refs, and advisory-only safety.
Phase 42 reviewer workbench binds claim revisions to explicit review decisions and follow-up workflow state without creating evidence or production authority.
Phase 43 reviewer workbench exports longitudinal reviewer history refs and hashes without raw history, evidence creation, or production authority.
